original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; UAE safeguarding law and pathways govern local use
original_registry_entry.population: Children and young people under 18, including unborn babies, who may be at risk of or experiencing abuse or neglect, and their parents or carers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Health, emergency, social-care and other services encountering children and families.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for child-centred communication, consent to examination, recording actual words, non-leading exploration, observing the child and relationships, and clinician-led safeguarding action. It does not assert abuse or replace UAE reporting requirements.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.9 — communication, consent and records
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.8 — recognising disclosure and responding
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recognising indirect disclosure, using open non-leading questions, explaining next steps and recording local safeguarding referral when clinician-completed.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.8 — assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports observing the child and interactions, communicating directly and privately where appropriate, non-leading exploration and structured family or care-environment context.

title: Child abuse and neglect
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; UAE safeguarding law and pathways govern local use
population: Children and young people under 18, including unborn babies, who may be at risk of or experiencing abuse or neglect, and their parents or carers.
intended_setting: Health, emergency, social-care and other services encountering children and families.
